{{Quote|Greetings. I am HG-211. I will be taking over your tour now. You will come with me, please.|HG-211, to the Heroes of Cularin, during their tour of X2-4 in about 31 BBY|<ref name="Something Uffel" />}}
'''HG-211''' was a [[Battle droid/Legends|military droid]] with a [[Gender/Legends|masculine]] persona who served as the head of security for a [[Uffel droid community|droid community]] that dwelled on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Uffel]] in the [[Cularin system/Legends|Cularin system]] during the waning [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Circa [[31 BBY/Legends|31 BBY]], the rogue [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] [[LN-73]] fell to the surface of Uffel during a debris storm and set about [[Saboteur/Legends|sabotaging]] [[Droid/Legends|droid]] production facilities in the [[City/Legends|city]] [[X2-4]]. HG-211 hired the [[Heroes of Cularin]], a group of freelance [[Agent/Legends|agents]], to identify the source of the vandalism, and after LN-73 learned of the military droid's investigation, it incapacitated HG-211 via a power surge from a [[Computer/Legends|computer]] terminal. However, the agents defeated LN-73, and HG-211 was repaired.

About [[20 BBY/Legends|eleven years later]], [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] from the [[Thaereian military]], an armed force that was active in the Cularin [[Star system/Legends|system]], invaded Uffel and occupied a production line in X2-4. The threat of the Thaereians led to the activation of protocol within the [[Programmer/Legends|programming]] of half of the Uffel droids, which caused them to become hostile to organic beings. HG-211 became the leader of a faction of loyalist droids that opposed the anti-organic droids, and he enlisted the help of the Heroes of Cularin to repulse the Thaereian invaders and to resolve the droid schism.

==Biography==
===Sabotage===
{{Quote|We are honored by your presence.|HG-211, to one of the Heroes of Cularin|<ref name="Something Uffel" />}}
HG-211 was a [[Battle droid/Legends|military droid]]<ref name="Something Uffel" /> with [[Gender/Legends|masculine programming]] who resided on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Uffel]] in the [[Cularin system/Legends|Cularin system]] during the waning [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. HG-211 served as the head of security for a [[Uffel droid community|community of droids]] that operated a series of [[Droid/Legends|droid]] production facilities on the moon,<ref name="LFCG" /> and he maintained an office in the [[City/Legends|city]] [[X2-4]].<ref name="Something Uffel" /> In about [[31 BBY/Legends|31 BBY]],<ref name="31 BBY">According to the ''[[Living Force Campaign Guide]]'', the [[Living Force (roleplaying campaign)|''Living Force'']] campaign is set a [[Standard year/Legends|year]] after the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]], an event that is set in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. Therefore, ''[[Broken Orbits: Something Uffel|Something Uffel]]'' is dated to about [[31 BBY/Legends|31 BBY]].</ref> a debris storm over Uffel caused the ancient [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] [[LN-73]] to fall to the surface of the moon. The droid had first been dispatched to Uffel about half a century earlier by the investor [[Hesk Beldross]] to take over the moon's droid production facilities, and it initiated its plans to to subjugate Uffel by [[Saboteur/Legends|sabotaging]] the moon's manufacturing lines, including a line producing [[MSF mouse droid|MSF-series]] droids, a variant of the [[MSE-series]] mouse droid. Following the sabotage, HG-211 ordered that all droids working on the MSF production line be fitted with [[Restraining bolt/Legends|restraining bolts]], to prevent them from affecting other droids.<ref name="Something Uffel" />

Shortly after LN-73's reactivation, the droids of Uffel ordered a halt in their production schedule to allow groups of tourists to visit the moon. The protocol droid [[LV-15]] was appointed to escort one such group of visitors that included the [[Heroes of Cularin]], a team of renowned freelance [[Agent/Legends|agents]] who operated in the [[Star system/Legends|system]]. As LV-15 led the tourists into X2-4, HG-211 dismissed the protocol droid and took over the escort of the tourist group. He led the agents to the office and played them a [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]] of [[Gor Kolomo]], a [[Death/Legends|deceased]] [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] under whose name the droids of Uffel conducted their business affairs, and the hologram hired the agents to investigate the recent acts of sabotage. HG-211 provided the agents with [[Comlink/Legends|comlinks]] and translator units to assist with their investigations, and he introduced them to the [[IT-series]] droid [[IT-R9]], which was to follow the agents around on behalf of the droids of the [[Uffel Research and Development]] facility, who sought to study the behavior of organic beings.<ref name="Something Uffel" />

Once the agents had investigated X2-4's production lines and droid development facilities for evidence of the identity of the saboteur, HG-211 contacted the agents via comlink and summoned them back to his office to explain their findings to him. However, LN-73 had learned of HG-211's investigation, and, while the [[Security droid/Legends|security droid]] was interviewing the agents, the rogue mechanical initiated a [[Power cell/Legends|power cell]] overload that caused a surge at HG-211's [[Computer/Legends|computer]] terminal and incapacitated the security droid. The Heroes of Cularin proceeded to locate and defeat LN-73, and HG-211 was taken away to be fixed. Two [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]] later, the repairs on the security droid were complete, and HG-211 thanked the agents for their services. He paid them a sum of [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] and bequeathed to them the droid [[MSF-111]], an MSF-series unit that had developed a fondness for the agents during the course of their investigation.<ref name="Something Uffel" />

===Thaereian incursion===
{{Quote|Shortly after the invasion, it became apparent that there was more going on. Certain droids started behaving oddly, leaving their designated posts to take up positions around the Thaereian incursion, showing familiarity with weapons not considered 'normal' to their programming.|HG-211, to the Heroes of Cularin|<ref name="Uffel's Prisoners">{{LivingForce|Inside-out: Uffel's Prisoners}}</ref>}}
Circa [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]],<ref name="20 BBY">The ''Living Force Campaign Guide'' states that the ''Living Force'' campaign is set one year after the Invasion of Naboo, an event that is dated to 32 BBY by ''The New Essential Chronology''. As the ''Living Force'' adventure ''[[Below the Belt: Philology|Philology]]'' [[Cularin's disappearance|moved]] the campaign forward ten years to the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], and the adventure ''[[Night Eyes: Night's Homecoming|Night's Homecoming]]'' established that by Year 4 of the campaign over a year had elapsed since the events of ''Philology'', ''[[Inside-out: Uffel's Prisoners|Uffel's Prisoners]]'' can therefore be dated to circa [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]].</ref> during the era of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the [[Thaereian military]]<ref name="Uffel's Prisoners" />—a force from the [[Thaereian system]] that was charged by the Republic with the defense of the Cularin system<ref name="Over Thaere">{{Wizards|url=rpga/livingforce/article.asp?x=lf/swlf20011004b_thaere&c=swlfc|text=Over Thaere}}</ref>—became engaged in an [[Thaereian War|armed conflict]] with the rival [[Cularin Militia]]. In an attempt to subvert the droids of Uffel to their cause, Thaereian forces landed on the moon to try to seize control of the droid production facilities. HG-211 led a security team that endeavored to repulse the invaders, but, to counter his attack, the Thaereians deployed [[Ion blaster/Legends|ion guns]],<ref name="Uffel's Prisoners" /> a type of handheld weapon that could disable the electrical systems of droids.<ref name="Galaxy at War">''[[Galaxy at War]]''</ref> As a result, HG-211 was unable to defeat the Thaereians, and the [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] occupied a military droid production line in X2-4.<ref name="Uffel's Prisoners" />

The Thaereian incursion led to the activation of hidden protocols within the [[Programmer/Legends|programming]] of about half of the droids on Uffel, which caused them to become hostile to all organic beings. A number of the rogue droids barricaded themselves inside the X2-4 Research and Development facility, and HG-211, who was not programed with the anti-organic protocols, had some of his [[Technician/Legends|technicians]] examine one of the insurrectionist droids. HG-211 soon became the leader of a loyalist droid faction that, while opposed to the Thaereian encroachment, had nothing against organic beings in general, and he established a headquarters for himself inside a security post in X2-4. He began searching for ways to reverse the protocols and to repel the forces of the Thaereians from Uffel, and he feared that if news of the anti-organic programming reached the [[The galaxy/Legends|wider galaxy]], it would cause a public relations crisis for the Uffel droids.<ref name="Uffel's Prisoners" />

The [[Cularin/Legends|Cularin]] Militia received word of the Thaereian invasion and sent the Heroes of Cularin to restore order to Uffel, and the agents arrived on the moon a [[Standard week/Legends|week]] after the initial Thaereian attack. Word of the agents' presence reached HG-211, and he sent the loyalist protocol droid [[OO-7JB]] to direct them to his headquarters. The security droid appealed to the agents to help repulse the Thaereians and to remove the hostile droid protocols, and the agents agreed to assist him.<ref name="Uffel's Prisoners" />

{{RPG|''[[Inside-out: Uffel's Prisoners|Uffel's Prisoners]]''}}
The Thaereian military learned of the anti-organic protocols within the programming of the Uffel droids, and they attempted to transmit a signal that would activate the protocols within all Uffel-built droids across the entire Cularin system. However, the Heroes of Cularin prevented the transmission from being sent and they defeated the Thaereian forces on the moon. To commend their efforts, HG-211 relayed a note of thanks to the Cularin Militia.<ref name="Uffel's Prisoners" />
{{Endgame}}

==Characteristics==
{{Quote|I have no particular fondness for organics, it seems obvious that droids are superior in many ways, but I wish you no ill will either.|HG-211|<ref name="Uffel's Prisoners" />}}
HG-211 was a tall, lanky, military droid<ref name="Something Uffel" /> who was similar in appearance to an early [[IG-series/Legends|IG-series]] droid.<ref name="Uffel's Prisoners" /> He had a gunmetal [[Color/Legends|gray]]-colored torso that was crisscrossed with thick, black stripes. A thin, red wire crossed one of the stripes and denoted a [[Self-destruct mechanism/Legends|self-destruct mechanism]] that was contained within the droid. Only HG-211 had knowledge of how to activate the system, and he was prepared to destroy himself if doing so aided the defense of the Uffel droid colony. In addition, HG-211 was armed with two mounted [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistols]], and he was fitted with a [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]] package, a [[Motion sensor/Legends|motion detector]], and [[Infrared/Legends|infrared]] vision.<ref name="LFCG" />

Despite his role as a military droid, HG-211 was programed with a diplomacy mode that, when activated, caused him to behave in a perfunctory manner. He was not particularly fond of organic beings and believed that droids were superior to them in a number of respects, yet he wished organics no ill.<ref name="Uffel's Prisoners" /> After the production facilities on Uffel were sabotaged, HG-211 found it difficult to believe that a droid could be responsible, and, when LN-73 was found to be behind the damage, HG-211 felt grumpy and slightly humiliated, because his assertions had been proved incorrect. He played a direct role in the running of Uffel's droid manufacturing facilities,<ref name="Something Uffel" /> and, following the Thaereian invasion, he became concerned that the anti-organic protocols displayed by some of the Uffel droids would generate a public relations crisis for the droid community, because the protocols threatened to undermine confidence in the reliability of Uffel-built droids.<ref name="Uffel's Prisoners" /> The droid [[Administrator/Legends|administrator]] [[N-4PO]] thought HG-211 to be brutish and believed that the security droid had no sense of what was in the best interests of the droids of Uffel.<ref name="Something Uffel" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|We will begin working on it immediately. We can fix it, I have no doubt, but it will take time. Weeks, at least.|HG-211, to Major Xirossk, if the player characters fail to prevent that Thaereian military from activating the anti-organic protocols in droids across the Cularin system|<ref name="Uffel's Prisoners" />}}
HG-211 was first mentioned in the ''[[Living Force Campaign Guide]]'' in [[2001]], a sourcebook written by [[Robert Wiese]] and [[Andy Collins]] that set the scene for the [[Living Force (roleplaying campaign)|''Living Force'']] [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game|roleplaying]] campaign.<ref name="LFCG" /> HG-211 went on to appear in the [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying adventure]] ''[[Broken Orbits: Something Uffel|Something Uffel]]'', by [[Morrie Mullins (real person)|Morrie Mullins]], in the first [[Standard year/Legends|year]] of the campaign,<ref name="Something Uffel" /> and he made a further appearance in the adventure ''[[Inside-out: Uffel's Prisoners|Uffel's Prisoners]]'', by [[Rob Edwards]], in [[2004]].<ref name="Uffel's Prisoners" /> The security droid later received an entry in the [[2008]] compendium ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|II|39|HG-211}}</ref>

The players of the ''Living Force'' campaign roleplay as the Heroes of Cularin, and, during ''Something Uffel'' and ''Uffel's Prisoners'', the decisions made by the players can affect the actions of HG-211.<ref name="Something Uffel" /><ref name="Uffel's Prisoners" /> In ''Something Uffel'', if the player characters choose to modify or reprogram any of the droids in X2-4, or to fire upon any of the city's MSF droids, HG-211 arrives at the scene with a team of warrior droids to stop them. During the player characters' subsequent investigation into the acts of sabotage caused by LN-73, it is possible for the players to discover that a worm in the computer systems of X2-4 is feeding information to a [[Satellite/Legends|satellite]] in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] of Uffel. If they inform HG-211 of such a discovery, the security droid immediately makes his way to the fire controls for X2-4's military defense systems, and he blasts the satellite from orbit. Another possibility allows players to choose to attempt to connect [[Datapad/Legends|datapads]] or other devices to the droids under production in X2-4; if they do so, the droids with which they have tampered shut down. If three or more droids are deactivated in such a manner, HG-211 arrives and berates the agents for not being attentive to their assignment. However, the agents can then negotiate with HG-211 for permission to continue connecting to the droids, and to try to convince him that gathering data in such a way is beneficial to the player characters' investigations.<ref name="Something Uffel" />

If the player characters used in ''Uffel's Prisoners'' have previously participated in the events of ''Something Uffel'', HG-211 is pleased to see such characters again and engages in his [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomacy]] mode. Depending on the player characters' skills and expertise, in ''Uffel's Prisoners'' HG-211 can task the agents with resolving the droid schism or with using their combat abilities to drive out the Thaereians, or he can request that they attempt both tasks. If the players fail to prevent the Thaereians from activating the anti-organic protocols within droids across the Cularin system, there are various droid revolts through the system, and the Cularin Militia intelligence chief [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Xirossk]] travels to Uffel to enlist HG-211's help with countering the rogue droids. HG-211 assures Xirossk that the loyalist droids can resolve the problem but warns that it will take several weeks for the renegade droids to be pacified.<ref name="Uffel's Prisoners" />
